data_leakage_policy: Ground-truth LLR text is filtered to the target allocation, used only by local evaluation/refinement, and is never inserted into generation prompts or the final prompt.
initial_prompt:
Create low-level requirements for exactly one target subsystem.

Scope rules:
- Generate only singular, atomic low-level requirements that belong to the target subsystem.
- Do not generate requirements for any other subsystem, even if the HLR could involve them.
- Every returned allocation value must be exactly "{TARGET_ALLOCATION}".
- Return at least one low-level requirement for the target subsystem.

Return JSON only, with no Markdown or explanatory prose, using exactly this schema:
{"low_level_requirements":[{"allocation":"{TARGET_ALLOCATION}","requirement":"requirement text"}]}

Allocation catalogue:
- CAS = Configuration & Administration Service
- CAUI = Configuration & Administration User Interface
- CS = Collector Services
- DOG = Watchdog
- EMC = Environmental Metadata Cache
- EMS = Environmental Metadata Services
- MEUI = Manual Entry User Interface
- QEDC = Qualified Environmental Data
- QEDS = Qualified Environmental Data Services
- QChS = Quality Checking Services
- SS = Schedule Service

High-level requirement:
{HLR}

Target allocation code: {TARGET_ALLOCATION}
Target subsystem: {TARGET_SUBSYSTEM}

High-level requirement:
The Clarus system shall implement quality checking processes as soon as data become available.

Target allocation code: CAS
Target subsystem: Configuration & Administration Service

low_level_requirements.requirement: CAS shall provide a configuration parameter specifying the maximum allowable delay between data becoming available and the initiation of quality checking processes.
CAS shall allow an administrator to enable automatic initiation of quality checking processes upon data availability.
CAS shall allow an administrator to disable automatic initiation of quality checking processes upon data availability.
CAS shall validate administrator-entered configuration values for quality checking initiation triggers prior to saving them.
CAS shall persist the configured trigger settings governing initiation of quality checking processes.
CAS shall notify the Quality Checking Services subsystem whenever configuration settings for data-triggered quality checking initiation are updated.
CAS shall maintain an audit record of all changes made to the configuration settings controlling quality checking process initiation.
CAS shall restrict modification of quality checking initiation trigger settings to authorized administrator roles.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The Quality Checking Services shall detect when new data becomes available.

low_level_requirements.requirement: The CAS shall provide a configuration mechanism for defining quality checking methods that can be applied to environmental data.
The CAS shall store the identity of each quality checking method configured for use in the Clarus system.
The CAS shall associate configured quality checking methods with the quality checking information they are used to derive.
The CAS shall maintain a record of which quality checking method was applied for each instance of derived quality checking information.
The CAS shall persist the recorded quality checking method information for later retrieval.
The CAS shall provide a means to retrieve the recorded quality checking method associated with a given instance of derived quality checking information.

The Collector Services shall transmit only data that has changed since the last successful transfer.
The Collector Services shall reuse established network connections for successive data transfers instead of creating new connections.
The Collector Services shall limit the size of each data transmission to the minimum required to convey the collected data.
The Collector Services shall discard duplicate data records prior to transmission.
The Collector Services shall buffer collected data locally when a transmission destination is unavailable, to avoid redundant transmission attempts.
The Collector Services shall retry a failed data transmission using an exponential backoff interval to avoid unnecessary network load.
The Collector Services shall close a data transmission connection immediately upon completion of the transfer.
The Collector Services shall schedule non-time-critical data transfers during periods of lower network utilization.
The Collector Services shall encode collected data using a minimal-overhead data format prior to transmission.
The Collector Services shall verify successful receipt of transmitted data before discarding the corresponding locally buffered copy.
